back_translation: 'Surely there is a mine for silver and a place where gold is refined.
Iron is taken from the earth, and copper is smelted from ore.
Man puts an end to the darkness and searches for ore to the deepest recesses, in deep darkness.
He digs a deep shaft far from human dwellings, in places forgotten by the foot of man; far from people he dangles and sways.
The earth, from which food grows, is overturned below as though by fire.
Its rocks are the source of sapphires, and flecks of gold are mixed in them.
No bird of prey knows that path, and no falcon's eye has ever seen it.
Proud beasts have never trodden it, and no lion has ever passed over it.
The miner strikes the flint and overturns mountains at their roots.
He cuts channels in the rock, and his eyes see every precious thing.
He dams up the sources of the streams and brings what is hidden out to light.
But where can wisdom be found, and where does understanding dwell?
No man knows its value, and it cannot be found in the land of the living.
The deep ocean says, 'Wisdom is not in me,' and the sea says, 'It is not with me.'
Wisdom cannot be bought with gold, nor can its price be weighed out in silver.
It cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir, nor in precious onyx or sapphire.
Gold and crystal cannot compare to it, nor can it be exchanged for ornaments of fine gold.
Coral and crystal are not worth mentioning; the price of wisdom is higher than rubies.
Topaz from Cush cannot compare to it, nor can it be valued in pure gold.
So where does wisdom come from, and where does understanding dwell?
Wisdom is hidden from the eyes of all living things and concealed from the birds of the air.
Abaddon and Death say, 'We have only heard a rumor of it with our ears.'
But God understands the way to wisdom, and He knows its place.
For He looks to the ends of the earth and sees everything under the heavens.
When He fixed the weight of the wind and measured out the waters,
when He set a limit for the rain and a path for the thunderbolt,
then He examined wisdom and appraised it; He established it and searched it out.
And He said to man, 'Behold, the fear of the Lord—that is wisdom, and to turn from evil is understanding.'
